channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Mediator Pakistan says renewed US-Iran conflict is ‘in no one’s interest’
item.description: Islamabad urges both sides to honour their MoU as renewed attacks raise fears of a wider regional conflict.

item.title: UK Labour leadership nominations begin: Who’s running and how it works
item.description: Former Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham is the frontrunner. If no rival emerges, he could become PM this month.

item.title: India floods sweep away thousands of gas cylinders
item.description: India floods sweep away thousands of gas cylinders

item.title: Deadly US strikes trigger Iranian attacks on Gulf states
item.description: The US military says it has struck 90 targets in Iran. Iran says it sent drone strikes on US-linked sites in the Gulf.

item.title: Watch World Cup Preview: Morocco plays France in quarterfinal opener
item.description: Latest match highlights, reaction and previews from the FIFA World Cup 2026 in North America.

item.title: Iran prepares to bury slain leader Khamenei after mass funeral processions
item.description: The late supreme leader will be buried in his hometown, the eastern holy city of Mashhad.

item.title: US strikes Iran for second night – is the peace process all over now?
item.description: Neither side has officially called off the interim peace agreement, despite the escalation.

item.title: UN probe finds mass killings, gang rapes by Sudan’s RSF amount to genocide
item.description: UN Fact-Finding Mission found that the paramilitary's systematic campaign of violence in Darfur amounted to genocide.

item.title: Australia to begin selling uranium to India for peaceful purposes
item.description: Australia to begin selling uranium to India for peaceful purposes

item.title: Air raid sirens in Bahrain after uneasy night of US-Iran strikes
item.description: After a tense night of overnight US-led strikes on Iran, air raid alerts sounded across Gulf states.

item.title: Who is Zeynep Sonmez, Wimbledon star with a ‘Palestine’ watermelon racket?
item.description: Turkish tennis player Zeynep Sonmez defies a Wimbledon ban to stand in solidarity with Palestine.

item.title: World Cup: France vs Morocco prediction, schedule, Hakimi vs Mbappe rivalry
item.description: Hakimi meets Mbappe as France face Morocco in the opening World Cup quarterfinal, with Opta backing Les Bleus to win.

item.title: Australia, India strike deal on uranium exports during PM Modi’s visit
item.description: India has long eyed Australia's uranium to help meet a target of 100 gigawatts of nuclear energy capacity by 2047.

item.title: US, Iran launch more attacks as mediators urge both sides to uphold MoU
item.description: Iran says it attacked sites linked to the US military in Kuwait, Bahrain and Qatar after US bombarded targets in Iran.

item.title: Hottest June on record in Western Europe as heatwaves hit continent
item.description: Thousands of deaths across Europe, mostly in France, Spain and Belgium, have been linked to a June heatwave.

item.title: Inside King’s College London’s crackdown on pro-Palestine students
item.description: King's College London, which has ties to the defence industry, has disciplined at least 26 students in two years.

item.title: ‘Shoot me, just let my son pass’: Final moments of Palestinian Ahmad Zaid
item.description: Three-month-old boy in critical condition dies after Israeli forces block his access to an ambulance.

item.title: Why are politicians and sanctioned states embracing crypto?
item.description: Bitcoin is falling, yet cryptocurrency is spreading beyond investing into politics, sanctions and global finance.

item.title: In maps and charts: South Sudan’s 15 years of independence
item.description: AJLabs breaks down the 15 years of liberation from Khartoum and the impact on 12 million people.

item.title: Morocco fans paint Boston red but remain wary of France at FIFA World Cup
item.description: On the eve of the quarterfinal, Morocco's supporters take over downtown Boston, showing unwavering belief in their team.

item.title: Fireworks as Morocco and France fans party ahead of World Cup match
item.description: Fireworks as Morocco and France fans party ahead of World Cup match

item.title: FIFA World Cup: Refereeing chief denies bias claims in Argentina-Egypt game
item.description: Pierluigi Collina said that such allegations of bias could provoke threats against referees and their families.

item.title: Flooding from Tropical Storm Maysak kills 39 in southern China
item.description: About 130,000 people have been evacuated from the Guangxi region while thousands of rescuers have been deployed.

item.title: Photos: Israeli attacks kill nine people, including two children, in Gaza
item.description: Israeli air attacks and gunfire kill nine Palestinians in Gaza despite ceasefire, medics say.

item.title: Lord’s Cricket Ground set for first-ever women’s Test as England host India
item.description: England host India in a women's Test at the iconic Lord's Cricket Ground - the first of such a kind at the venue.
